anchor.snippet: a quasigroup is an algebraic structure that resembles a group

annotations.label: Nonempty associative quasigroup is a group

anchor.snippet: a nonempty associative quasigroup is a group
A quasigroup that has an identity element is called a loop
A quasigroup ( Q , ∗) is a set Q with a binary operation ∗
The operations ' \ ' and ' / ' are called, respectively, left division and right division
The empty set equipped with the empty binary operation satisfies this definition of a quasigroup
Algebraic structures that satisfy axioms that are given solely by identities are called varieties
A right-quasigroup ( Q , ∗, /) is a type (2, 2) algebra that satisfies the identities
A left-quasigroup ( Q , ∗, \) is a type (2, 2) algebra that satisfies the identities
A quasigroup ( Q , ∗, \, /) is a type (2, 2, 2) algebra

A quasigroup with an idempotent element is called a pique
A loop that is associative is a group

A loop that is both a left and right Bol loop is a Moufang loop
A quasigroup is semisymmetric if any of the following equivalent identities hold
every quasigroup Q induces a semisymmetric quasigroup
a totally symmetric quasigroup (sometimes abbreviated TS-quasigroup ) in which all conjugates coincide
Idempotent total symmetric quasigroups are precisely (i.e. in a bijection with) Steiner triples
A quasigroup ( Q , ∗) is called weakly totally anti-symmetric
A quasigroup ( Q , ∗) is called totally anti-symmetric
Every group is a loop
The integers Z (or the rationals Q or the reals R ) with subtraction
The nonzero rationals Q × (or the nonzero reals R × ) with division
Any vector space over a field of characteristic not equal to 2 forms an idempotent
Every Steiner triple system defines an idempotent , commutative quasigroup

annotations.label: Nonassociative loop of order 8 (hyperbolic quaternion units)

anchor.snippet: The set {±1, ±i, ±j, ±k} where ii = jj = kk = +1

annotations.label: Nonzero octonions form a Moufang loop

anchor.snippet: The nonzero octonions form a nonassociative loop under multiplication

annotations.label: Associative quasigroup is empty or a group

anchor.snippet: An associative quasigroup is either empty or is a group
The following construction is due to Hans Zassenhaus

annotations.label: Nonzero elements of a division algebra

anchor.snippet: the nonzero elements of any division algebra form a quasigroup
Quasigroups have the cancellation property

anchor.snippet: The Latin square property of quasigroups implies that, given any two of the three variables

annotations.label: Magma is quasigroup iff multiplication operators are bijective

anchor.snippet: A magma Q is a quasigroup precisely when all these operators

annotations.label: Multiplication table of finite quasigroup is a Latin square

anchor.snippet: The multiplication table of a finite quasigroup is a Latin square

annotations.label: Latin squares give quasigroup multiplication tables

anchor.snippet: every Latin square can be taken as the multiplication table of a quasigroup in many ways

annotations.label: Latin square property for infinite quasigroups

anchor.snippet: the Latin square property says that each row and each column of the infinite array will contain every possible value precisely once
The binary operation of a quasigroup is invertible

annotations.label: Left and right inverse in a loop

anchor.snippet: Every loop element has a unique left and right inverse
A loop is said to have ( two-sided ) inverses

annotations.label: Inverse property loops (LIP, RIP, AAIP, WIP)

anchor.snippet: There are some stronger notions of inverses in loops that are often useful
A loop has the inverse property if it has both the left and right inverse properties

annotations.label: One-sided inverse properties imply two-sided inverses

anchor.snippet: Any loop that satisfies the left, right, or antiautomorphic inverse properties automatically has two-sided inverses
A quasigroup or loop homomorphism is a map
A quasigroup homotopy from Q to P is a triple
An isotopy is a homotopy for which each of the three maps
An autotopy is an isotopy from a quasigroup to itself

annotations.label: Every quasigroup is isotopic to a loop

anchor.snippet: Every quasigroup is isotopic to a loop
Every medial quasigroup is isotopic to an abelian group by the Bruck–Toyoda theorem

An n - ary quasigroup is a set with an n -ary operation
A 0-ary, or nullary , quasigroup is just a constant element of Q

annotations.label: Iterated group operation as multiary quasigroup

anchor.snippet: An example of a multiary quasigroup is an iterated group operation
An n -ary quasigroup is irreducible if its operation cannot be factored
An n -ary quasigroup with an n -ary version of associativity is called an n -ary group
